findings.description: yaml.load is called without SafeLoader, allowing arbitrary Python object construction and remote code execution from a malicious config file.
Precedence is inverted: env is updated before file, so file values overwrite env, contradicting the documented 'env > file > defaults' contract.
On PyYAML >= 6.0 yaml.load without a Loader argument raises TypeError, breaking config loading entirely at runtime.
get/require/feature_enabled each call load(), re-reading the file from disk, re-scanning os.environ, and re-parsing YAML on every single-key access with no caching.
open() and yaml parsing are unguarded, so a permission error or malformed YAML (yaml.YAMLError) propagates as an unhandled exception despite the missing-file case being handled.
feature_enabled assumes feature_flags is a dict, but an env/file override can make it a scalar, causing AttributeError on .get.
Env keys are flattened to lowercase scalars, so APP_FEATURE_FLAGS replaces the nested feature_flags dict with a string and there is no namespacing for nested values.
Because load() runs on every accessor, the INFO 'config loaded' line is emitted repeatedly, producing log spam under normal use.
_coerce aggressively converts all-digit strings to int (e.g. zip codes, tokens, leading-zero values), silently changing intended string types.
require uses get with a None default and cannot distinguish a key explicitly set to YAML null (present) from an absent key, raising KeyError for legitimately-present null values.
TOCTOU between os.path.exists and open allows the file to disappear between the check and the read.
Override values for worker_count and request_timeout_s are not type-validated, so a string from file/env can flow into integer-expecting call sites.
Mixes typing.Optional with PEP 585 builtin generics (dict[str, Any]); could use 'Any | None' for consistency.
